upgraded_system_overview: Enterprise-grade collaborative meeting platform optimized for academic institutions with premium UI/UX. Combines WebRTC P2P for small groups with optional SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) for scalability. Introduces persistence layer (chat, files, recordings), comprehensive security (passwords, waiting rooms, role-based permissions), and academic features (lecture mode, attendance tracking, participation analytics). Modern glassmorphic UI with Framer Motion animations inspired by top SaaS platforms.

new_architecture.media_layer: Hybrid P2P + SFU architecture: Default P2P for ≤6 participants (bandwidth efficient), automatically upgrade to mediasoup SFU for >6 participants. Participants can enable audio-only fallback for low-bandwidth scenarios. Adaptive bitrate negotiation per peer connection. Server-side media recording via FFmpeg/GStreamer integration.
new_architecture.realtime_layer: Enhanced Socket.IO with namespace segregation (/meeting/:code for isolated rooms), exponential backoff reconnection (3s → 30s max), presence awareness (user online/offline status), typing indicators, and read receipts for chat. Event versioning for backward compatibility.
new_architecture.storage_layer: MySQL for relational data (meetings, users, chat, attendance, permissions). MinIO/S3 for file/recording storage (hybrid: local disk for ≤100MB, cloud for larger). Redis for session caching and real-time state (optional, for horizontal scaling). Message queue (Bull/RabbitMQ) for async tasks (email notifications, recording processing).

improvements: Messages persisted to database with sender_id, meeting_id, timestamp
Message editing and deletion with audit trail
Actual file uploads (not base64) via multipart/form-data
File sharing permissions (host only, all participants)
Multiple recording modes: audio-only, video-only, combined
Automatic transcoding to multiple formats (MP4, WebM)
Host can see queue order and accept/reject individual hands
Analytics on hand raises per participant
Audio/video isolated per breakout room (not shared with main room)
Host can visit each breakout room
Automatic participant assignment or manual join
Time warnings (5 min, 1 min before end)
Show meeting status (scheduled, in-progress, ended, recording)
Quick action buttons (join, start, edit, delete)

new_features_added.description: Meetings can require password or one-time token. Host sets password when creating meeting. Participants enter password to join.
new_features_added.socket: join-meeting event validates password before admission

new_features_added.description: When enabled, participants join waiting room first. Host reviews and manually admits. Waiting room has its own video preview and chat.
Four roles: host (full control), co-host (moderation + recording), moderator (mute/unmute, remove), participant (basic). Permissions matrix in database.
Host/moderator can remove participant from meeting. Can optionally ban from future meetings.
Host can enable lecture mode where all participants are muted by default. Only host speaks. Participants can request unmute via hand raise.
Auto-log join/leave timestamps for all participants. Attendance report shows duration, entry/exit times, presence %.)
new_features_added.database: attendance_logs table with user_id, meeting_id, join_time, leave_time

new_features_added.description: Detailed stats per participant: speaking time, hand raises, messages sent, reactions, files shared.
new_features_added.tracking: Track events in meeting_events table (speak, message, reaction, file_share)

new_features_added.description: Visual queue showing ordered list of raised hands. Host can approve/reject/mute individual participants.
Async email reminders for upcoming meetings (1 day, 1 hour before). Join alerts for host when participant joins.
new_features_added.implementation: Bull job queue + nodemailer or SendGrid

new_features_added.description: Participants with poor bandwidth can switch to audio-only mode, reducing CPU/bandwidth by 80%.
new_features_added.media: Disable video tracks, disable screen share

new_features_added.description: Monitor packet loss and RTT, dynamically adjust video resolution (1080p → 720p → 360p) and framerate.
new_features_added.implementation: Monitor REMB (Receiver Estimated Maximum Bitrate) feedback, adjust constraints in RTCPeerConnection

new_features_added.description: Post-meeting report with attendees, duration, participation breakdown, recordings, chat export.
OAuth2/LTI endpoints for Moodle, Canvas, Blackboard integration. Auto-sync course enrollments.

design_principles: Glassmorphism: Frosted glass effect (backdrop-filter: blur, semi-transparent backgrounds)
Minimalism: Remove clutter, focus on essential actions
Micro-interactions: Smooth transitions (Framer Motion), hover effects, loading states
Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
Dark-first: Base color #0f1117 with subtle gradients
Consistency: Design tokens, reusable component library

sections: Header: Logo, user profile dropdown, notifications icon
Sidebar: Collapsible nav (Dashboard, Meetings, History, Settings), user stats card
Main: Meeting grid/list with cards, quick-start CTA, stats cards
Floating: Plus button for new meeting
Top bar: Meeting title, recording indicator, participant count, leave button
Main grid: Video tiles with active speaker highlight
Bottom controls: Floating control bar (mute, video, share, reactions)
Right panel: Tabbed sidebar (chat, participants, files, hand-raise queue)
Modals: Settings, breakout rooms, waiting room
meeting_room.responsive: Stacked layout on mobile, collapsible right panel

key_components.name: WaitingRoom
features: List of pending users with admit/reject buttons

interaction_patterns.example: Click mute → button turns red immediately → audio track disabled → other users see 'muted' badge
Hover over mute button → tooltip 'Mute microphone (M)' appears after 300ms
Participant joins → name slides in from bottom with fade-in (0.3s cubic-bezier)
Send reaction 👍 → emoji floats up screen with scale-in + fade-out animation (2.5s total)
Meeting list loading → show 3 skeleton cards with pulse animation → replace with real data on load
User joins → toast 'John joined' appears top-right for 3s with slide-in animation
Delete message → toast with 'Undo' button appears for 5s

ui_ux_design_system.animation_strategy: Framer Motion library with presets: enter (fadeInUp 0.3s), exit (fadeOutDown 0.2s), hover (scale 1.05), tap (scale 0.95). Stagger animations for lists. Spring animations for bouncy feedback.

meeting_room_ui_structure.top_bar: Fixed height 60px, background glassmorphic, contains: (left) meeting code + title + recording status dot, (center) participant count + network status, (right) floating controls (screenshare, chat toggle for mobile). Color scheme: #16181f with border-bottom 1px solid #23262f.
meeting_room_ui_structure.video_grid: Responsive CSS grid, auto-fit columns (minmax 240px, 1fr), aspect-ratio 16/9. Active speaker highlighted with 4px inset glow (#818cf8). Video tiles have glassmorphic name badge bottom-left, hand raise emoji top-right, mute indicator top-left. Gap between tiles: 12px. Padding: 20px. Mobile: single column stacked.
meeting_room_ui_structure.control_bar: Floating bar position fixed bottom-center, transform translateX(-50%), left 50%, margin-bottom 20px. Background: glassmorphic #16181f with border 1px #23262f. Display: flex gap-8px, padding 12px 20px, border-radius 16px. Buttons: square 44px with icons, hover scale 1.1 + shadow, active scale 0.95. Order: Mic | Video | Screenshare | Hand | Emoji picker | Chat toggle | Settings | Leave (red).
meeting_room_ui_structure.side_panel: Fixed right 0 top 60px bottom 0, width 320px (mobile: full-screen overlay). Background: #16181f border-left 1px #23262f. Contains: (top) Tab bar (Chat, Participants, Files, Hand Queue), (middle) content area with scroll, (bottom) input area (for chat) or action buttons. Tab icons: 💬 Chat (count badge), 👥 Participants, 📁 Files, ✋ Hand Queue. Tab styling: flex 1, text-center, padding 12px, border-bottom 2px solid transparent, active: #818cf8.

modals.name: WaitingRoomModal
modals.layout: Center modal, width 460px max, background glassmorphic. Title: 'Waiting for Admission'. Content: video preview from webcam (aspect 16:9, border-radius xl), message 'You are in the waiting room...', actions: none (just wait or leave).

modals.name: BreakoutRoomsModal
modals.layout: Center modal, width 520px. Title: 'Breakout Rooms'. (Host view) input numRooms, duration, 'Create Rooms' button. (Active view) grid 2 columns of room cards, each showing room name, member count, action button (Join or Leave). Timer countdown top-right.

modals.name: SettingsModal
modals.layout: Center modal, width 460px. Tabs: Audio/Video, Meeting, Accessibility. Toggle controls, dropdowns for device selection. Save/Cancel buttons.

modals.name: RecordingModal
modals.layout: Alert-style modal, 'Recording has started. Recording indicator visible in top bar. Auto-close after 3s or click OK.

ADD INDEX (meeting_status) for filtering upcoming meetings

api_changes.purpose: Enhanced join with password validation and waiting room flow
Host admits participant from waiting room
Host denies participant from waiting room
Update meeting configuration (password, lecture mode, recording)
Send chat message (deprecated, use Socket.IO instead)

socket_changes.enhancements: Validate password if meeting protected, check waiting room setting, return status (joined | waiting)

socket_changes.purpose: User admitted from waiting room → connect WebRTC
User rejected from waiting room → disconnect

socket_changes.enhancements: Include message_id, is_edited flag, auto-persist to DB

socket_changes.purpose: All participants muted by default, can request unmute via hand raise
WebRTC offer for SFU connection (when >6 participants)

current_limitations: In-memory state: Breakout rooms, chat, presence → lost on server restart
Single server: No horizontal scaling, session affinity issues
P2P only: Bandwidth inefficient for >6 participants, creates bottleneck
Browser recording: Only host's stream, stored locally, loss of data
No database persistence: Chat, files, recordings → all in-memory
Rate limiting: None → vulnerable to abuse
File storage: No file uploads handling → base64 bloat

solutions.solution: Implement Redis cache layer for session state (breakout rooms, presence, chat queue). Periodic sync to MySQL. Implementation: ioredis library, cache TTL 1h.
Use Socket.IO Redis adapter for multi-server deployment. Sessions sticky-routed via load balancer or Redis pubsub. Requires Docker + reverse proxy (Nginx).
Detect participant count, auto-upgrade to mediasoup SFU when >6 participants. SFU forwards media from each peer to all others (star topology). Reduce bandwidth by 60-80% for large meetings.
Server-side recording via FFmpeg subprocess. Store in MinIO (S3-compatible). Async job queue (Bull) for encoding/transcoding. Webhook callback on completion.
Write to MySQL immediately (async insert). Archive old messages to separate table or S3 after 90 days. Implement pagination (fetch 50 messages at a time).
Implement express-rate-limit middleware. Limits: 100 requests/min per IP, 50 messages/min per user, 10 files/meeting. Block repeat offenders.
Use multer middleware for multipart/form-data. Store files in MinIO (local S3-compatible server or AWS S3). Generate thumbnails via sharp. CDN for delivery.
Track granular events in meeting_events table (speak, message, reaction). Use materialized views or scheduled jobs to pre-compute analytics. Export via API.
Use coturn TURN server pool (load-balanced). Participants connect to nearest TURN relay. Improves NAT traversal for geo-distributed users.

security_improvements.improvement: Implement JWT refresh tokens with short expiry (15 min access, 7d refresh). Blacklist revoked tokens in Redis.
security_improvements.implementation: POST /api/auth/refresh endpoint, store blacklist in Redis

security_improvements.improvement: Meeting password stored as bcrypt hash. One-time tokens for guest access (email invite links).
security_improvements.implementation: Compare input hash, generate 32-char random token with 24h expiry

security_improvements.improvement: Role-based access control (RBAC) enforced server-side on all APIs. Check permission before allowing action (mute, remove, record).
security_improvements.implementation: Middleware: checkPermission(action, meeting_id) on each protected endpoint

security_improvements.improvement: Sanitize chat messages (XSS prevention), validate file uploads (MIME type, size limits), escape SQL queries (Sequelize parameterization).
security_improvements.implementation: joi/yup schemas for request validation, DOMPurify for chat HTML, multer fileFilter

security_improvements.improvement: Prevent brute force on login, limit message spam, limit file uploads.
Encrypt meeting passwords and sensitive data at rest (bcrypt passwords, AES for meeting tokens).
security_improvements.implementation: crypto library, store encrypted in DB

security_improvements.improvement: Enforce HTTPS in production, use valid SSL certificates (not self-signed), enable HSTS header.
Strict CORS origin validation, CSP headers, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options.
Log all sensitive actions (login, kick, record start, password change) with IP and timestamp.
Soft delete for user accounts, encrypt user emails, GDPR-ready data export/deletion APIs.
security_improvements.implementation: timestamps for deleted_at, encrypt PII, implement data portability endpoints

academic_use_case_support.lecture_mode: Instructor enables lecture mode when starting meeting. All students automatically muted (audio + video disabled). Only instructor visible by default. Students can request to unmute via hand raise → instructor sees queue → can approve individual or all. Recording continues throughout, auto-saved to course. Perfect for large lectures (100+ students) where only instructor speaks.
academic_use_case_support.student_interaction: Hand raise queue creates orderly participation. Students see their position in queue. Instructor can accept hands in order or skip. Chat available for questions (if enabled by instructor). Reactions (thumbs up, clapping) provide quick non-verbal feedback without unmuting. Attendance auto-tracked → instructor sees who joined and for how long.
academic_use_case_support.assessment_support: Meeting recordings stored and linked to course (LMS integration ready). Instructor can export meeting transcript (via speech-to-text plugin). Chat exported as CSV. Attendance report downloadable. Analytics show student engagement metrics (hand raises, messages, speaking time). Ready for integration with assignment submission (students can reference recording as source material).
